title: WASM Feature Surface Slimming
problem: Merman's current core and browser-WASM dependency surfaces are too broad for non-JS WebAssembly hosts such as Typst plugins.
gates: cargo run -p xtask -- profile-budget check-deps --profile pure-wasm --package merman-core --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --no-default-features --depth 3
cargo run -p xtask -- profile-budget check-wasm --profile typst-wasm --wasm <typst-probe.wasm>
cargo nextest run -p merman-core -p merman-render -p merman-bindings-core

evidence.note: Typst plugins use wasmi and the wasm-minimal-protocol import surface, not wasm-bindgen browser glue.
Parser-only local probe produced a 4.3 MB wasm with 12 wasm-bindgen/js-sys imports, including WebCrypto and JS Date.
Current browser wasm-size default produced an 8.5 MB wasm with 49 wasm-bindgen/js imports; no-default produced 2.7 MB with 25 imports.

WFS-060 split full YAML frontmatter and JSON5 directives into full-config; merman-core --no-default-features no longer links serde_yaml, unsafe-libyaml, json5, or pest while preserving common inline metadata parsing.
WFS-070 split DOMPurify-like HTML rewriting and URL canonicalization into full-sanitization; no-default core no longer links lol_html, url, ICU/idna URL dependencies, or browser/host crates.

Typst bridge-only stripped wasm is 33,412 bytes; default render stripped wasm is 5,414,863 bytes; core-full is 6,262,880 bytes; ratex-math is 8,238,005 bytes. Default render size is now dominated by real code/data, not custom metadata.

WFS-080 made detector, semantic parser, typed render parser, and supported metadata projections profile-aware from BaselineRegistryProfile; tiny/no-default excludes mindmap, architecture, and flowchart-elk parser/render registrations.

WFS-090 started with `xtask wasm-size-matrix`, which builds browser and Typst feature presets separately and reports raw plus stripped wasm bytes. Full matrices are recorded in PACKAGE_SURFACES.md and EVIDENCE_AND_GATES.md without conflating wasm-bindgen browser packages with Typst/pure-wasm packages.
